title: vachellia campeachiana f. houghii
intro: Vachellia campeachiana f. houghii, also known as the Hough's palo fiero, is a sub-species of the Vachellia campeachiana tree, native to the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bark and leaves of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and wounds, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties and is used to treat a range of health conditions.
